business_definition: Represents a formal request submitted by a policyholder for compensation following an insured event.
Unique business identifier assigned to the claim.
Official claim number assigned by the insurer.
Current operational status of the claim.
Classification of the submitted claim.
Business classification of the claim.
Date on which the claim was registered.
Business priority assigned to the claim.
Channel through which the claim was initiated.
Simplified explanation of the claim.
example: Own damage claim for front bumper collision.

business_definition: Represents the First Notice of Loss (FNOL) submitted by the policyholder.
Timestamp when FNOL was received.
Channel used for reporting the loss.
Classification of person reporting the claim.
Initial description of the reported incident.
Indicates whether emergency support was requested.
Indicates whether a police report exists.
Indicates whether towing assistance was requested.
Represents completeness of submitted FNOL information.
Simplified explanation of the FNOL.
Represents the insured incident giving rise to the claim.
Classification of the insured event.
Date on which the insured event occurred.
Time of occurrence of the insured event.
Location where the insured event occurred.
Weather conditions during the event.
Road condition during the event.
Indicates involvement of another party.
Indicates whether injuries occurred.
Simplified explanation of the insured event.
Represents technical assessment of insured damages.
Classification of damage.
Vehicle component affected by the loss.
Business severity assigned to the damage.
Estimated repair cost before survey approval.
Represents whether repair is technically feasible.
Indicates whether the vehicle is considered a total loss.
Current inspection workflow status.
Simplified explanation of inspection findings.
Overall explanation of assessed damages.
Represents verification that the reported loss is covered under the issued policy.
Current status of coverage validation.
Canonical coverage applicable to the reported damage.
Decision indicating whether the loss is payable.
Business reason explaining why coverage was denied.
Represents validation of policy status during claim processing.
Verification that claim amount is within policy limits.
Verification of applicable deductible.
Simplified explanation of coverage validation.
example: Damage covered under Own Damage coverage.

business_definition: Confidence associated with automated coverage validation.
Represents assessment performed by an authorized surveyor.
Unique identifier assigned to the survey.
Reference to the assigned surveyor.
Date on which vehicle inspection occurred.
Technical findings recorded during inspection.
Repair estimate approved by the surveyor.
Recommendation issued by the surveyor.
Current survey workflow status.
Simplified explanation of survey findings.
Confidence associated with survey assessment.
Represents financial settlement of an approved insurance claim.
Unique identifier assigned to settlement.
Amount approved by the insurer.
Amount payable by the insured.
Depreciation deducted from settlement.
Final amount payable to the claimant.
Mode through which settlement is completed.
Current settlement workflow status.
Date on which settlement was completed.
Simplified explanation of settlement outcome.
Represents recoveries and salvage associated with a settled claim.
Indicates whether recovery action is applicable.
Amount recoverable from third parties.
Indicates whether salvage exists.
Estimated value of recoverable salvage.
Indicates whether subrogation applies.
Current recovery workflow status.
Current salvage processing status.
Simplified explanation of recovery activities.
Final business outcome of recovery activities.
Represents the operational lifecycle of the insurance claim.
Current lifecycle stage of the claim.
Assignment status of claim handler.
Indicates whether claim has been escalated.
Indicates whether claim is closed.
Date of final claim closure.
Total processing time for the claim.
Indicates whether claim processing met SLA targets.
Simplified explanation of claim lifecycle.
Chronological summary of important claim events.
Represents fraud analysis associated with the claim.
Composite fraud risk score.
Classification of fraud risk.
Indicates whether fraud investigation is recommended.
Collection of fraud indicators identified during processing.
Overall business and AI assessment of the claim.
Confidence associated with the overall claim assessment.
Natural-language explanation supporting the claim decision.
example: Approve claim based on verified coverage and survey.

business_definition: Version of the claim assessment methodology.
Overall explanation of claim processing outcome.
